summary.title: AEP Operator Catalog Admission Policy Hardening
summary.description: Validation of OperatorCatalogAdmissionPolicy hardening features including approval status validation, side-effecting operator validation, commit SHA binding, and evidence policy requirements. Ensures operators admitted to the catalog meet production truth and governance requirements.

operatorCatalogAdmissionPolicy.purpose: Runtime admission policy for cataloged operators that blocks unapproved or unsafe operator use at catalog admission time

hardeningFeatures.description: Validates that operators have approved status before runtime use.
hardeningFeatures.implementation: requireApproved() checks metadata for approvalStatus field. Must be 'approved' (case-insensitive).
validationRules: Denied or missing approval status is rejected
hardeningFeatures.testCoverage: ApprovalStatusValidation nested test class with 4 tests

hardeningFeatures.description: Validates that side-effecting operators have tool policy declared.
hardeningFeatures.implementation: requireApproved() checks sideEffectProfile for SIDE_EFFECTING and requires toolPolicyDeclared metadata.
validationRules: Non-side-effecting operators do not require tool policy
toolPolicyDeclared must be 'true' for side-effecting operators
hardeningFeatures.testCoverage: SideEffectingOperatorValidation nested test class with 3 tests

hardeningFeatures.description: Binds operator admission to commit SHA for production truth tracking.
hardeningFeatures.implementation: validateProductionConstraints() checks commitSha presence, format, and match with operator metadata commitSha.
validationRules: Production environment requires non-null, non-empty commit SHA
Operator metadata must include commitSha in production
Operator commitSha must match deployment commit SHA
Non-production environments do not require commit SHA
hardeningFeatures.testCoverage: CommitShaBinding nested test class with 6 tests

hardeningFeatures.description: Requires evidence policy configuration in production.
hardeningFeatures.implementation: validateProductionConstraints() checks for evidencePolicy metadata in production environment.
validationRules: Non-production environments do not require evidence policy
hardeningFeatures.testCoverage: EvidencePolicyValidation nested test class with 3 tests

hardeningFeatures.implementation: validateProductionConstraints() is called when environment is 'production'. Combines commit SHA and evidence policy validation.
validationRules: Production validation only applies when environment is 'production'
All production constraints must pass for admission
hardeningFeatures.testCoverage: Production constraints are tested through commit SHA and evidence policy tests

testCategories.name: Approval Status Validation
testCategories.coverage: Rejects without approval status, rejects with denied status, accepts with approved status, accepts with uppercase APPROVED

testCategories.name: Side-Effecting Operator Validation
testCategories.coverage: Rejects side-effecting without tool policy, accepts side-effecting with tool policy, allows non-side-effecting without tool policy

testCategories.name: Commit SHA Binding
testCategories.coverage: Production requires commit SHA, production accepts valid commit SHA, production rejects invalid format, production requires operator metadata commit SHA, production validates commit SHA match, non-production does not require commit SHA

testCategories.name: Evidence Policy Validation
testCategories.coverage: Production requires evidence policy, production accepts evidence policy, non-production does not require evidence policy

unifiedOperatorCatalog.purpose: Unified operator catalog with pluggable discovery strategies

pipelineStageExecutor.purpose: Pipeline stage executor with admission policy integration

productionTruthGuarantees: Operators are bound to specific commit SHA in production
Commit SHA format is validated (40-character hexadecimal)
Operator metadata commit SHA must match deployment commit SHA
Evidence policy is required in production for audit trail
Approval status is validated for all operators
Production constraints are enforced at admission time
securityConsiderations: Commit SHA binding prevents deployment of unverified operators
Approval status ensures only approved operators run
Tool policy validation prevents unauthorized side effects
Evidence persistence ensures audit trail in production
Commit SHA match prevents operator version drift
notes: Commit SHA binding is enforced for production environment only
Approval status validation ensures only approved operators run
Side-effecting operators require tool policy for safety
Evidence policy is required in production for audit trail
Test coverage includes all admission policy hardening features
Production truth is maintained through commit SHA and evidence validation
Admission policy is integrated with UnifiedOperatorCatalog and PipelineStageExecutor
